text: Joseph told his steward to fill the men's sacks with as much food as they could carry and put each man's silver in his sack.
Put my silver cup in the mouth of the youngest one's sack, along with his grain silver; the steward did as Joseph said.
As morning dawned, the men were sent on their way with their donkeys.
They had not gone far when Joseph told his steward to pursue them and ask why they repaid good with evil.
Isn't this the cup my master drinks from and uses for divination? You have done a wicked thing.
When the steward caught up, he repeated these words to them.
They said, why does my lord say such things? Far be it from your servants to do this.
We even brought back the silver we found in our sacks; why would we steal silver or gold from your master's house?
If any of your servants is found with it, he will die, and the rest of us will become my lord's slaves.
Very well, he said, let it be as you say; whoever is found with it will become my slave, the rest of you will be free.
Each of them quickly lowered his sack to the ground and opened it.
The steward searched, beginning with the oldest and ending with the youngest; the cup was found in Benjamin's sack.
At this they tore their clothes; then they all loaded their donkeys and returned to the city.
Judah and his brothers came to Joseph's house, and he was still there; they fell to the ground before him.
Joseph said, what is this you've done? Didn't you know a man like me can find things out by divination?
Judah said, what can we say to my lord? How can we prove our innocence? God has uncovered your servants' guilt; we are now my lord's slaves, all of us and the one with the cup.
Joseph said, far be it from me to do that; only the man with the cup will be my slave, the rest of you go back to your father in peace.
Judah approached him and said, please let your servant speak a word to my lord; do not be angry with your servant, though you are equal to Pharaoh himself.
My lord asked us if we had a father or brother.
We answered we have an aged father and a young brother born to him in his old age; his brother is dead, and he alone is left of his mother's children, and his father loves him.
Then you said to us, bring him down to me so I can see him.
We said, the boy cannot leave his father; if he leaves, his father would die.
But you told your servants, unless your youngest brother comes with you, you will not see my face again.
When we went back to your servant my father, we told him what my lord had said.
Then our father said, go back and buy a little more food.
We said, we cannot go unless our youngest brother goes with us; we cannot see the man's face without our youngest brother.
Your servant my father said to us, you know my wife bore me two sons.
One of them went away and I said he'd surely been torn to pieces, and I have not seen him since.
If you take this one from me too and harm comes to him, you will bring my gray head down to the grave in misery.
So if the boy is not with me when I go to your servant my father, and his life is bound up with the boy's life,
when he sees the boy is not with us, he will die; your servants will bring down our father's gray head in sorrow to the grave.
Your servant guaranteed the boy's safety to my father, saying if I don't bring him back I will bear the blame before my father forever.
Now let your servant remain here as my lord's slave in place of the boy, and let him return with his brothers.
How can I go back to my father if the boy is not with me? I couldn't bear to see the misery that would come upon my father.
